1â†’# Claims Processing System â€” Implementation Plan
     2â†’
     3â†’## Context
     4â†’
     5â†’Building a complete insurance claims processing system from scratch. The system allows members to submit claims for reimbursement, auto-adjudicates them against plan coverage rules and deductible accumulators, tracks the claim through a lifecycle state machine, and supports a single-round dispute mechanism.     58â†’## Phase 1: Project Scaffolding
    59â†’
    60â†’### `pyproject.toml`

    64â†’version = "0.1.0"

    66â†’dependencies = [
    67â†’    "flask>=3.1.0",
    68â†’    "flask-sqlalchemy>=3.1.1",
    69â†’]
    70â†’
    71â†’[tool.uv]
    72â†’dev-dependencies = [
    73â†’    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    74â†’    "pytest-flask>=1.3.0",
    75â†’    "mypy>=1.13.0",
    76â†’    "ruff>=0.8.0",
    77â†’]
    78â†’
    79â†’[tool.ruff]
    80â†’target-version = "py313"
    81â†’line-length = 100
    82â†’
    83â†’[tool.ruff.lint]
    84â†’select = ["E", "F", "I", "UP"]
    85â†’
    86â†’[tool.mypy]
    87â†’python_version = "3.13"
    88â†’strict = true
    89â†’plugins = ["sqlalchemy.ext.mypy.plugin"]
    90â†’```
    91â†’
    92â†’### `config/settings.py`
    93â†’```python
    94â†’import os
    95â†’
    96â†’class Config:
    97â†’    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///claims.db")
    98â†’    SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS = False
    99â†’
   100â†’class TestingConfig(Config):
   101â†’    TESTING = True
   102â†’    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = "sqlite:///:memory:"
   103â†’```
   104â†’
   105â†’### `app/extensions.py`
   106â†’```python
   107â†’from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
   108â†’db: SQLAlchemy = SQLAlchemy()
   109â†’```
   110â†’
   111â†’### `app/__init__.py` â€” App factory
   112â†’```python
   113â†’from flask import Flask
   114â†’from config.settings import Config
   115â†’from .extensions import db
   116â†’from .routes import register_routes
   117â†’from .errors import register_error_handlers
   118â†’
   119â†’def create_app(config_object: object = Config) -> Flask:
   120â†’    app = Flask(__name__, static_folder="static")
   121â†’    app.config.from_object(config_object)
   122â†’    db.init_app(app)
   123â†’    with app.app_context():
   124â†’        db.create_all()
   125â†’    register_routes(app)
   126â†’    register_error_handlers(app)
   127â†’    return app
   128â†’```
   129â†’
   130â†’---
   131â†’
   132â†’## Phase 2: Models
   133â†’
   134â†’### `app/models.py`
   135â†’
   136â†’All enums and models live in this single file. At 12 models this is manageable (~500 lines), avoids circular import issues entirely (all classes are in scope simultaneously so relationships use direct class references, not strings), and guarantees every model is registered before `db.create_all()` is called.
   137â†’
   138â†’**Enums** â€” defined at the top of the file:
   139â†’
   140â†’```python
   141â†’import enum
   142â†’
   143â†’class ProviderType(enum.Enum):
   144â†’    individual = "individual"
   145â†’    facility = "facility"
   146â†’
   147â†’class PolicyStatus(enum.Enum):
   148â†’    active = "active"
   149â†’    expired = "expired"
   150â†’    cancelled = "cancelled"
   151â†’
   152â†’class ClaimStatus(enum.Enum):
   153â†’    submitted = "submitted"
   154â†’    under_review = "under_review"
   155â†’    approved = "approved"
   156â†’    denied = "denied"
   157â†’    partially_approved = "partially_approved"
   158â†’    paid = "paid"
   159â†’
   160â†’class ReviewType(enum.Enum):
   161â†’    auto = "auto"
   162â†’    manual = "manual"
   163â†’
   164â†’class LineItemStatus(enum.Enum):
   165â†’    pending = "pending"
   166â†’    approved = "approved"
   167â†’    denied = "denied"
   168â†’
   169â†’class DisputeStatus(enum.Enum):
   170â†’    pending = "pending"
   171â†’    resolved = "resolved"
   172â†’```
   173â†’
   174â†’**Model classes** â€” defined below the enums in this order (respects FK dependencies top-down):
   175â†’`Member` â†’ `Provider` â†’ `Plan` â†’ `CoverageRule` â†’ `Policy` â†’ `Accumulator` â†’ `Claim` â†’ `ClaimStatusHistory` â†’ `AdjudicationResult` â†’ `LineItem` â†’ `Dispute` â†’ `Payment`
   176â†’
   177â†’> **Circular FK note:** `LineItem.latest_result_id` â†’ `AdjudicationResult` and `AdjudicationResult.line_item_id` â†’ `LineItem` form a mutual FK reference. Resolve with `use_alter=True` on `LineItem.latest_result_id` so SQLAlchemy emits it as a post-creation `ALTER TABLE` rather than failing on the forward reference.
   178â†’
   179â†’### Model Specifications
   180â†’
   181â†’All UUIDs stored as `String(36)` with `default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())`.
   182â†’All monetary amounts stored as `Numeric(10, 2)` mapped to Python `Decimal`.
   183â†’All models carry `deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None]` except `ClaimStatusHistory` and `AdjudicationResult` (append-only, no soft delete).
   184â†’
   185â†’**Member** â€” `id, name, date_of_birth(Date), email(unique), phone(nullable), created_at, deleted_at`
   186â†’Relationships: `policies`, `claims`, `accumulators`
   187â†’
   188â†’**Provider** â€” `id, name, npi(unique), provider_type(ProviderType enum), created_at, deleted_at`
   189â†’
   190â†’**Plan** â€” `id, name, deductible(Numeric 10,2), created_at, deleted_at`
   191â†’Relationships: `coverage_rules`, `policies`
   192â†’
   193â†’**CoverageRule** â€” `id, plan_id(FK), cpt_code(str), is_covered(bool), coverage_percentage(Numeric 5,4), created_at, deleted_at`
   194â†’UniqueConstraint: `(plan_id, cpt_code)` on active rules only â€” enforced at service layer since SQLite lacks partial indexes without triggers.
   195â†’
   196â†’**Policy** â€” `id, member_id(FK), plan_id(FK), start_date(Date), end_date(Date), status(PolicyStatus), created_at, deleted_at`
   197â†’Constraint: at most one `active` policy per member â€” enforced at service layer.
   198â†’Relationships: `member`, `plan`, `claims`, `accumulator`
   199â†’
   200â†’**Accumulator** â€” `id, member_id(FK), policy_id(FK unique), deductible_met(Numeric 10,2 default 0.00), created_at, updated_at, deleted_at`
   201â†’UniqueConstraint: `(member_id, policy_id)`. One row per policy period per member.
   202â†’
   203â†’**Claim** â€” `id, member_id(FK), policy_id(FK), provider_id(FK), date_of_service(Date), status(ClaimStatus default submitted), review_type(ReviewType default auto), submitted_at, updated_at, deleted_at`
   204â†’Relationships: `member`, `policy`, `provider`, `line_items`, `status_history`, `dispute`, `payment`
   205â†’
   206â†’**ClaimStatusHistory** â€” `id, claim_id(FK), from_status(ClaimStatus nullable), to_status(ClaimStatus), transitioned_at, note(nullable)`
   207â†’No `deleted_at`. Append-only. Full audit trail per AC Â§3.
   208â†’
   209â†’**LineItem** â€” `id, claim_id(FK), diagnosis_code(str), cpt_code(str), billed_amount(Numeric 10,2), adjudication_status(LineItemStatus default pending), latest_result_id(FK â†’ AdjudicationResult nullable), updated_at, deleted_at`
   210â†’`latest_result_id` is a foreign key back to `AdjudicationResult` â€” avoids correlated subqueries on reads (Design Note Â§3).
   211â†’
   212â†’**AdjudicationResult** â€” `id, line_item_id(FK), revision(int), is_covered(bool), applied_to_deductible(Numeric 10,2), plan_pays(Numeric 10,2), member_owes(Numeric 10,2), explanation(Text), adjudicated_at`
   213â†’UniqueConstraint: `(line_item_id, revision)`. No `deleted_at`. Append-only. Re-adjudication adds revision N+1, never mutates.
   214â†’
   215â†’**Dispute** â€” `id, claim_id(FK unique â€” one per claim), reason(Text), reviewer_note(Text nullable), submitted_at, resolved_at(nullable), status(DisputeStatus default pending), deleted_at`
   216â†’One-dispute rule enforced by presence of this row, not a counter on Claim (Design Note Â§5 from domain-model).
   217â†’
   218â†’**Payment** â€” `id, claim_id(FK unique), amount(Numeric 10,2), paid_at, deleted_at`
   219â†’Created automatically when claim transitions to `paid`.
   220â†’
   221â†’---
   222â†’
   223â†’## Phase 3: Error Handling
   224â†’
   225â†’### `app/errors.py`
   226â†’
   227â†’```python
   228â†’class ClaimsError(Exception):
   229â†’    status_code: int = 500
   230â†’    error_code: str = "INTERNAL_ERROR"
   231â†’
   232â†’class BadRequestError(ClaimsError):  # 400, "BAD_REQUEST"   â€” malformed input, missing/invalid fields
   233â†’class NotFoundError(ClaimsError):    # 404, "NOT_FOUND"
   234â†’class ValidationError(ClaimsError):  # 422, "VALIDATION_ERROR" â€” valid input, business rule violation
   235â†’class ConflictError(ClaimsError):    # 409, "CONFLICT"      â€” operation invalid for current resource state
   236â†’class ForbiddenError(ClaimsError):   # 403, "FORBIDDEN"
   237â†’```
   238â†’
   239â†’**When to use each:**
   240â†’- `BadRequestError` (400): missing required fields, zero/negative billed amounts, empty line_items list â€” the request itself is malformed.
   241â†’- `ValidationError` (422): structurally valid request that fails a business rule â€” e.g. no active policy covers the date of service, date of service outside policy period.
   242â†’- `ConflictError` (409): the resource exists and is valid, but its current state prevents the operation â€” e.g. disputing a claim that is already disputed, operating on a claim in the wrong status.
   243â†’
   244â†’`register_error_handlers(app)` handles all `ClaimsError` subclasses and generic Flask 404/405/500, always returning:
   245â†’```json
   246â†’{"error": "ERROR_CODE", "message": "Human-readable message"}
   247â†’```
   248â†’
   249â†’---
   250â†’
   251â†’## Phase 4: Adjudication Engine (Critical â€” TDD)
   252â†’
   253â†’### `app/services/adjudication_engine.py`
   254â†’
   255â†’**Contract:** `AdjudicationEngine.run(claim_id: str) -> Claim`
   256â†’
   257â†’All DB writes occur in a **single transaction** (Design Note Â§1 from domain-model):
   258â†’
   259â†’1. Lock `Accumulator` row with `with_for_update()` â€” prevents concurrent double-spend (AC Â§8)
   260â†’2. Transition claim `submitted â†’ under_review`, `INSERT ClaimStatusHistory`
   261â†’3. For each active `LineItem` (ordered by id for determinism):
   262â†’   - Reset: `adjudication_status = pending`, `latest_result_id = None`
   263â†’   - Lookup `CoverageRule` for `(plan_id, cpt_code)` where `deleted_at IS NULL`
   264â†’   - If no rule or `is_covered=False` â†’ **denied result**
   265â†’   - Else â†’ **approved result**: apply adjudication math with `effective_deductible_remaining = plan.deductible - accumulator.deductible_met - total_consumed_this_run`
   266â†’   - `INSERT AdjudicationResult` (revision = max existing + 1, or 1 if none)
   267â†’   - `db.session.flush()` to get result.id
   268â†’   - Update `LineItem.latest_result_id`, `adjudication_status`
   269â†’4. `UPDATE Accumulator.deductible_met += total_deductible_consumed`
   270â†’5. Derive `ClaimStatus` from line item outcomes:
   271â†’   - All approved â†’ `approved` | All denied â†’ `denied` | Mix â†’ `partially_approved`
   272â†’6. Transition `under_review â†’ <final_status>`, `INSERT ClaimStatusHistory`
   273â†’7. If `approved`: create `Payment(amount=sum(plan_pays))`, transition to `paid`
   274â†’8. If `partially_approved` AND resolved `Dispute` exists (re-adjudication path): create `Payment`, transition to `paid`
   275â†’9. `db.session.commit()`
   276â†’
   277â†’**Adjudication Math (per line item):**
   278â†’```python
   279â†’remaining = plan.deductible - accumulator.deductible_met - total_consumed_so_far
   280â†’applied_to_deductible = min(billed_amount, max(Decimal("0.00"), remaining))
   281â†’amount_after_deductible = billed_amount - applied_to_deductible
   282â†’plan_pays = (amount_after_deductible * coverage_percentage).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), ROUND_HALF_UP)
   283â†’member_owes = (billed_amount - plan_pays).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), ROUND_HALF_UP)
   284â†’```
   285â†’
   286â†’**Explanation generation:** Built deterministically inline â€” no external calls, no failure modes:
   287â†’```python
   288â†’# Denied
   289â†’explanation = f"Service {cpt_code} is not covered under your plan."
   290â†’# Approved
   291â†’explanation = (
   292â†’    f"Service {cpt_code} covered at {int(coverage_percentage * 100)}%. "
   293â†’    f"${applied_to_deductible} applied to deductible. "
   294â†’    f"Plan pays ${plan_pays}; you owe ${member_owes}."
   295â†’)
   296â†’```
   297â†’
   298â†’---
   299â†’
   300â†’## Phase 5: Services
   301â†’
   302â†’### `app/services/claim_service.py`
   303â†’
   304â†’**`submit_claim(data: dict) -> Claim`**:
   305â†’1. Validate required fields: `member_id`, `provider_id`, `date_of_service`, `line_items` (non-empty; each must have `diagnosis_code`, `cpt_code`, `billed_amount > 0`) â€” **400** `BadRequestError` on any failure
   306â†’2. Load Member â€” 404 if not found or soft-deleted
   307â†’3. Find Policy where `member_id` matches, `status=active`, `start_date <= date_of_service <= end_date` â€” **422** `ValidationError` if none ("No active policy covers this date of service")
   308â†’4. Load Provider â€” 404 if not found
   309â†’5. Create `Claim(status=submitted, review_type=auto)` + `LineItem` rows
   310â†’6. Create `ClaimStatusHistory(from_status=None, to_status=submitted)`
   311â†’7. Upsert `Accumulator` for `(member_id, policy_id)` â€” create with `deductible_met=0.00` if first claim this period. Each `Policy` row has its own `Accumulator`; creating a new policy for a new period automatically produces a fresh accumulator â€” the old one is never zeroed, preserving history.
   312â†’8. `db.session.commit()`
   313â†’9. Call `AdjudicationEngine().run(claim.id)`
   314â†’10. Return refreshed claim
   315â†’
   316â†’### `app/services/dispute_service.py`
   317â†’
   318â†’**`submit_dispute(claim_id: str, reason: str) -> Dispute`**:
   319â†’1. Load Claim â€” 404 if not found
   320â†’2. Guard: `claim.status` must be `denied` or `partially_approved` â€” **409** `ConflictError` otherwise ("Cannot dispute a claim with status {status}")
   321â†’3. Guard: no existing `Dispute` row for this claim â€” **409** `ConflictError` if one exists ("Claim has already been disputed")
   322â†’4. Create `Dispute(reason=reason, status=pending)`
   323â†’5. Set `claim.review_type = manual`
   324â†’6. Transition claim to `under_review`, `INSERT ClaimStatusHistory`
   325â†’7. Reset all active LineItems: `adjudication_status=pending`, `latest_result_id=None`
   326â†’8. `db.session.commit()`
   327â†’
   328â†’**`trigger_readjudication(claim_id: str, reviewer_note: str | None) -> Claim`**:
   329â†’1. Load Claim â€” must be `under_review` with `review_type=manual` â€” **409** `ConflictError` otherwise ("Claim is not awaiting manual review")
   330â†’2. Load Dispute â€” 404 if no `Dispute` row exists; **409** `ConflictError` if dispute exists but `status != pending` ("Dispute is already resolved")
   331â†’3. Update `Dispute.reviewer_note` if provided
   332â†’4. Set `Dispute.status=resolved`, `Dispute.resolved_at=now()`
   333â†’5. `db.session.commit()`
   334â†’6. Call `AdjudicationEngine().run(claim.id)` â€” engine auto-pays if result is `partially_approved` (Dispute already resolved, no further dispute possible)
   335â†’7. Return refreshed claim
   336â†’
   337â†’**`accept_payment(claim_id: str) -> Payment`**:
   338â†’1. Load Claim â€” must be `partially_approved` â€” **409** `ConflictError` otherwise ("Cannot accept payment for a claim with status {status}")
   339â†’2. Guard: no `Dispute` row exists â€” **409** `ConflictError` if one does ("Dispute already filed; cannot accept partial payment")
   340â†’3. Create `Payment(amount=sum(plan_pays for approved line items))`
   341â†’4. Transition claim to `paid`, `INSERT ClaimStatusHistory`
   342â†’5. `db.session.commit()`
   343â†’
   344â†’---
   345â†’
   346â†’## Phase 6: API Routes
   347â†’
   348â†’All endpoints return `application/json`. Errors: `{"error": "CODE", "message": "..."}`.
   349â†’
   350â†’| Method | Path | Handler | Response |
   351â†’|--------|------|---------|----------|
   352â†’| POST | `/api/members` | create member | 201 |
   353â†’| GET | `/api/members/<id>` | get member | 200 / 404 |
   354â†’| GET | `/api/members/<id>/claims` | list member claims (summary) | 200 |
   355â†’| GET | `/api/members/<id>/policies/active` | get active policy | 200 / 404 |
   356â†’| POST | `/api/providers` | create provider | 201 |
   357â†’| GET | `/api/providers/<id>` | get provider | 200 / 404 |
   358â†’| POST | `/api/plans` | create plan + coverage rules | 201 |
   359â†’| GET | `/api/plans/<id>` | get plan + active rules | 200 / 404 |
   360â†’| PUT | `/api/plans/<id>/coverage-rules/<cpt_code>` | upsert rule (soft-delete old, insert new) | 200 |
   361â†’| DELETE | `/api/plans/<id>/coverage-rules/<cpt_code>` | soft-delete rule | 204 |
   362â†’| POST | `/api/policies` | create policy (creates Accumulator) | 201 |

# Claims Processing System â€” Implementation Plan

## Context

Building a complete insurance claims processing system from scratch. The system allows members to submit claims for reimbursement, auto-adjudicates them against plan coverage rules and deductible accumulators, tracks the claim through a lifecycle state machine, and supports a single-round dispute mechanism. The stack is Flask + SQLite + SQLAlchemy + Vanilla JS.

---

## Directory Structure

```
claims-processing-system/
â”śâ”€â”€ app/
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ __init__.py                  # create_app() factory
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ extensions.py                # db = SQLAlchemy()
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ models.py                    # All enums and models in one file
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ routes/
â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ __init__.py              # register_routes(app)
â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ members.py               # Blueprint /api/members
â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ providers.py             # Blueprint /api/providers
â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ plans.py                 # Blueprint /api/plans
â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ policies.py              # Blueprint /api/policies
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ claims.py                # Blueprint /api/claims (includes disputes + payments)
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ services/
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ __init__.py
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ claim_service.py         # Claim submission + retrieval
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ adjudication_engine.py   # Core adjudication logic (transactional)
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ dispute_service.py       # Dispute submission + re-adjudication trigger
â”śâ”€â”€ tests/
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ conftest.py                  # App, db, client, seed-data fixtures
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ unit/
â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ test_adjudication_engine.py  # Math + deductible tracking (mirrors adjudication_engine.py)
â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ test_claim_service.py        # Claim submission validation (mirrors claim_service.py)
â”‚   â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ test_dispute_service.py      # Dispute/re-adjudication guards (mirrors dispute_service.py)
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ test_state_machine.py        # Cross-service state transition coverage
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ integration/
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ test_members_api.py
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ test_plans_api.py
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ test_policies_api.py
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ test_claims_api.py
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ test_disputes_api.py

â”śâ”€â”€ app/static/
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ index.html                   # Dashboard (claim list + submission)
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ claim.html                   # Claim detail page
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ admin.html                   # Admin: plans, policies, re-adjudication
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ css/styles.css
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ js/
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ api.js                   # Fetch wrapper
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ dashboard.js
â”‚       â”śâ”€â”€ claim-detail.js
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ admin.js
â”śâ”€â”€ pyproject.toml
â””â”€â”€ .env.example
```

## Phase 1: Project Scaffolding

### `pyproject.toml`
```toml
[project]
name = "claims-processing-system"
version = "0.1.0"
requires-python = ">=3.13"
dependencies = [
    "flask>=3.1.0",
    "flask-sqlalchemy>=3.1.1",
]

[tool.uv]
dev-dependencies = [
    "pytest>=8.0.0",
    "pytest-flask>=1.3.0",
    "mypy>=1.13.0",
    "ruff>=0.8.0",
]

[tool.ruff]
target-version = "py313"
line-length = 100

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "I", "UP"]

[tool.mypy]
python_version = "3.13"
strict = true
plugins = ["sqlalchemy.ext.mypy.plugin"]
```

### `config/settings.py`
```python
import os

class Config:
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = os.environ.get("DATABASE_URL", "sqlite:///claims.db")
    SQLALCHEMY_TRACK_MODIFICATIONS = False

class TestingConfig(Config):
    TESTING = True
    SQLALCHEMY_DATABASE_URI = "sqlite:///:memory:"
```

### `app/extensions.py`
```python
from flask_sqlalchemy import SQLAlchemy
db: SQLAlchemy = SQLAlchemy()
```

### `app/__init__.py` â€” App factory
```python
from flask import Flask
from config.settings import Config
from .extensions import db
from .routes import register_routes
from .errors import register_error_handlers

def create_app(config_object: object = Config) -> Flask:
    app = Flask(__name__, static_folder="static")
    app.config.from_object(config_object)
    db.init_app(app)
    with app.app_context():
        db.create_all()
    register_routes(app)
    register_error_handlers(app)
    return app
```

---

## Phase 2: Models

### `app/models.py`

All enums and models live in this single file. At 12 models this is manageable (~500 lines), avoids circular import issues entirely (all classes are in scope simultaneously so relationships use direct class references, not strings), and guarantees every model is registered before `db.create_all()` is called.

**Enums** â€” defined at the top of the file:

```python
import enum

class ProviderType(enum.Enum):
    individual = "individual"
    facility = "facility"

class PolicyStatus(enum.Enum):
    active = "active"
    expired = "expired"
    cancelled = "cancelled"

class ClaimStatus(enum.Enum):
    submitted = "submitted"
    under_review = "under_review"
    approved = "approved"
    denied = "denied"
    partially_approved = "partially_approved"
    paid = "paid"

class ReviewType(enum.Enum):
    auto = "auto"
    manual = "manual"

class LineItemStatus(enum.Enum):
    pending = "pending"
    approved = "approved"
    denied = "denied"

class DisputeStatus(enum.Enum):
    pending = "pending"
    resolved = "resolved"
```

**Model classes** â€” defined below the enums in this order (respects FK dependencies top-down):
`Member` â†’ `Provider` â†’ `Plan` â†’ `CoverageRule` â†’ `Policy` â†’ `Accumulator` â†’ `Claim` â†’ `ClaimStatusHistory` â†’ `AdjudicationResult` â†’ `LineItem` â†’ `Dispute` â†’ `Payment`

> **Circular FK note:** `LineItem.latest_result_id` â†’ `AdjudicationResult` and `AdjudicationResult.line_item_id` â†’ `LineItem` form a mutual FK reference. Resolve with `use_alter=True` on `LineItem.latest_result_id` so SQLAlchemy emits it as a post-creation `ALTER TABLE` rather than failing on the forward reference.

### Model Specifications

All UUIDs stored as `String(36)` with `default=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4())`.
All monetary amounts stored as `Numeric(10, 2)` mapped to Python `Decimal`.
All models carry `deleted_at: Mapped[datetime | None]` except `ClaimStatusHistory` and `AdjudicationResult` (append-only, no soft delete).

**Member** â€” `id, name, date_of_birth(Date), email(unique), phone(nullable), created_at, deleted_at`
Relationships: `policies`, `claims`, `accumulators`

**Provider** â€” `id, name, npi(unique), provider_type(ProviderType enum), created_at, deleted_at`

**Plan** â€” `id, name, deductible(Numeric 10,2), created_at, deleted_at`
Relationships: `coverage_rules`, `policies`

**CoverageRule** â€” `id, plan_id(FK), cpt_code(str), is_covered(bool), coverage_percentage(Numeric 5,4), created_at, deleted_at`
UniqueConstraint: `(plan_id, cpt_code)` on active rules only â€” enforced at service layer since SQLite lacks partial indexes without triggers.

**Policy** â€” `id, member_id(FK), plan_id(FK), start_date(Date), end_date(Date), status(PolicyStatus), created_at, deleted_at`
Constraint: at most one `active` policy per member â€” enforced at service layer.
Relationships: `member`, `plan`, `claims`, `accumulator`

**Accumulator** â€” `id, member_id(FK), policy_id(FK unique), deductible_met(Numeric 10,2 default 0.00), created_at, updated_at, deleted_at`
UniqueConstraint: `(member_id, policy_id)`. One row per policy period per member.

**Claim** â€” `id, member_id(FK), policy_id(FK), provider_id(FK), date_of_service(Date), status(ClaimStatus default submitted), review_type(ReviewType default auto), submitted_at, updated_at, deleted_at`
Relationships: `member`, `policy`, `provider`, `line_items`, `status_history`, `dispute`, `payment`

**ClaimStatusHistory** â€” `id, claim_id(FK), from_status(ClaimStatus nullable), to_status(ClaimStatus), transitioned_at, note(nullable)`
No `deleted_at`. Append-only. Full audit trail per AC Â§3.

**LineItem** â€” `id, claim_id(FK), diagnosis_code(str), cpt_code(str), billed_amount(Numeric 10,2), adjudication_status(LineItemStatus default pending), latest_result_id(FK â†’ AdjudicationResult nullable), updated_at, deleted_at`
`latest_result_id` is a foreign key back to `AdjudicationResult` â€” avoids correlated subqueries on reads (Design Note Â§3).

**AdjudicationResult** â€” `id, line_item_id(FK), revision(int), is_covered(bool), applied_to_deductible(Numeric 10,2), plan_pays(Numeric 10,2), member_owes(Numeric 10,2), explanation(Text), adjudicated_at`
UniqueConstraint: `(line_item_id, revision)`. No `deleted_at`. Append-only. Re-adjudication adds revision N+1, never mutates.

**Dispute** â€” `id, claim_id(FK unique â€” one per claim), reason(Text), reviewer_note(Text nullable), submitted_at, resolved_at(nullable), status(DisputeStatus default pending), deleted_at`
One-dispute rule enforced by presence of this row, not a counter on Claim (Design Note Â§5 from domain-model).

**Payment** â€” `id, claim_id(FK unique), amount(Numeric 10,2), paid_at, deleted_at`
Created automatically when claim transitions to `paid`.

## Phase 3: Error Handling

### `app/errors.py`

```python
class ClaimsError(Exception):
    status_code: int = 500
    error_code: str = "INTERNAL_ERROR"

class BadRequestError(ClaimsError):  # 400, "BAD_REQUEST"   â€” malformed input, missing/invalid fields
class NotFoundError(ClaimsError):    # 404, "NOT_FOUND"
class ValidationError(ClaimsError):  # 422, "VALIDATION_ERROR" â€” valid input, business rule violation
class ConflictError(ClaimsError):    # 409, "CONFLICT"      â€” operation invalid for current resource state
class ForbiddenError(ClaimsError):   # 403, "FORBIDDEN"
```

**When to use each:**
- `BadRequestError` (400): missing required fields, zero/negative billed amounts, empty line_items list â€” the request itself is malformed.
- `ValidationError` (422): structurally valid request that fails a business rule â€” e.g. no active policy covers the date of service, date of service outside policy period.
- `ConflictError` (409): the resource exists and is valid, but its current state prevents the operation â€” e.g. disputing a claim that is already disputed, operating on a claim in the wrong status.

`register_error_handlers(app)` handles all `ClaimsError` subclasses and generic Flask 404/405/500, always returning:
```json
{"error": "ERROR_CODE", "message": "Human-readable message"}
```

## Phase 4: Adjudication Engine (Critical â€” TDD)

### `app/services/adjudication_engine.py`

**Contract:** `AdjudicationEngine.run(claim_id: str) -> Claim`

All DB writes occur in a **single transaction** (Design Note Â§1 from domain-model):

1. Lock `Accumulator` row with `with_for_update()` â€” prevents concurrent double-spend (AC Â§8)
2. Transition claim `submitted â†’ under_review`, `INSERT ClaimStatusHistory`
3. For each active `LineItem` (ordered by id for determinism):
   - Reset: `adjudication_status = pending`, `latest_result_id = None`
   - Lookup `CoverageRule` for `(plan_id, cpt_code)` where `deleted_at IS NULL`
   - If no rule or `is_covered=False` â†’ **denied result**
   - Else â†’ **approved result**: apply adjudication math with `effective_deductible_remaining = plan.deductible - accumulator.deductible_met - total_consumed_this_run`
   - `INSERT AdjudicationResult` (revision = max existing + 1, or 1 if none)
   - `db.session.flush()` to get result.id
   - Update `LineItem.latest_result_id`, `adjudication_status`
4. `UPDATE Accumulator.deductible_met += total_deductible_consumed`
5. Derive `ClaimStatus` from line item outcomes:
   - All approved â†’ `approved` | All denied â†’ `denied` | Mix â†’ `partially_approved`
6. Transition `under_review â†’ <final_status>`, `INSERT ClaimStatusHistory`
7. If `approved`: create `Payment(amount=sum(plan_pays))`, transition to `paid`
8. If `partially_approved` AND resolved `Dispute` exists (re-adjudication path): create `Payment`, transition to `paid`
9. `db.session.commit()`

**Adjudication Math (per line item):**
```python
remaining = plan.deductible - accumulator.deductible_met - total_consumed_so_far
applied_to_deductible = min(billed_amount, max(Decimal("0.00"), remaining))
amount_after_deductible = billed_amount - applied_to_deductible
plan_pays = (amount_after_deductible * coverage_percentage).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), ROUND_HALF_UP)
member_owes = (billed_amount - plan_pays).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), ROUND_HALF_UP)
```

**Explanation generation:** Built deterministically inline â€” no external calls, no failure modes:
```python
# Denied
explanation = f"Service {cpt_code} is not covered under your plan."
# Approved
explanation = (
    f"Service {cpt_code} covered at {int(coverage_percentage * 100)}%. "
    f"${applied_to_deductible} applied to deductible. "
    f"Plan pays ${plan_pays}; you owe ${member_owes}."
)
```

## Phase 5: Services

### `app/services/claim_service.py`

**`submit_claim(data: dict) -> Claim`**:
1. Validate required fields: `member_id`, `provider_id`, `date_of_service`, `line_items` (non-empty; each must have `diagnosis_code`, `cpt_code`, `billed_amount > 0`) â€” **400** `BadRequestError` on any failure
2. Load Member â€” 404 if not found or soft-deleted
3. Find Policy where `member_id` matches, `status=active`, `start_date <= date_of_service <= end_date` â€” **422** `ValidationError` if none ("No active policy covers this date of service")
4. Load Provider â€” 404 if not found
5. Create `Claim(status=submitted, review_type=auto)` + `LineItem` rows
6. Create `ClaimStatusHistory(from_status=None, to_status=submitted)`
7. Upsert `Accumulator` for `(member_id, policy_id)` â€” create with `deductible_met=0.00` if first claim this period. Each `Policy` row has its own `Accumulator`; creating a new policy for a new period automatically produces a fresh accumulator â€” the old one is never zeroed, preserving history.
8. `db.session.commit()`
9. Call `AdjudicationEngine().run(claim.id)`
10. Return refreshed claim

### `app/services/dispute_service.py`

**`submit_dispute(claim_id: str, reason: str) -> Dispute`**:
1. Load Claim â€” 404 if not found
2. Guard: `claim.status` must be `denied` or `partially_approved` â€” **409** `ConflictError` otherwise ("Cannot dispute a claim with status {status}")
3. Guard: no existing `Dispute` row for this claim â€” **409** `ConflictError` if one exists ("Claim has already been disputed")
4. Create `Dispute(reason=reason, status=pending)`
5. Set `claim.review_type = manual`
6. Transition claim to `under_review`, `INSERT ClaimStatusHistory`
7. Reset all active LineItems: `adjudication_status=pending`, `latest_result_id=None`
8. `db.session.commit()`

**`trigger_readjudication(claim_id: str, reviewer_note: str | None) -> Claim`**:
1. Load Claim â€” must be `under_review` with `review_type=manual` â€” **409** `ConflictError` otherwise ("Claim is not awaiting manual review")
2. Load Dispute â€” 404 if no `Dispute` row exists; **409** `ConflictError` if dispute exists but `status != pending` ("Dispute is already resolved")
3. Update `Dispute.reviewer_note` if provided
4. Set `Dispute.status=resolved`, `Dispute.resolved_at=now()`
5. `db.session.commit()`
6. Call `AdjudicationEngine().run(claim.id)` â€” engine auto-pays if result is `partially_approved` (Dispute already resolved, no further dispute possible)
7. Return refreshed claim

**`accept_payment(claim_id: str) -> Payment`**:
1. Load Claim â€” must be `partially_approved` â€” **409** `ConflictError` otherwise ("Cannot accept payment for a claim with status {status}")
2. Guard: no `Dispute` row exists â€” **409** `ConflictError` if one does ("Dispute already filed; cannot accept partial payment")
3. Create `Payment(amount=sum(plan_pays for approved line items))`
4. Transition claim to `paid`, `INSERT ClaimStatusHistory`
5. `db.session.commit()`

```javascript
const BASE = "/api";

async function request(method, path, body = null) {
    const opts = { method, headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } };
    if (body) opts.body = JSON.stringify(body);
    const res = await fetch(BASE + path, opts);
    if (!res.ok) {
        const err = await res.json();
        throw new Error(err.message || `HTTP ${res.status}`);
    }
    return res.status === 204 ? null : res.json();
}

export const api = {
    // Members
    createMember: (data) => request("POST", "/members", data),
    getMember: (id) => request("GET", `/members/${id}`),
    getMemberClaims: (id) => request("GET", `/members/${id}/claims`),
    getActivePolicyForMember: (id) => request("GET", `/members/${id}/policies/active`),

    // Providers
    createProvider: (data) => request("POST", "/providers", data),

    // Plans
    createPlan: (data) => request("POST", "/plans", data),
    getPlan: (id) => request("GET", `/plans/${id}`),
    upsertCoverageRule: (planId, cptCode, data) => request("PUT", `/plans/${planId}/coverage-rules/${cptCode}`, data),

    // Policies
    createPolicy: (data) => request("POST", "/policies", data),

    // Claims
    submitClaim: (data) => request("POST", "/claims", data),
    getClaim: (id) => request("GET", `/claims/${id}`),
    submitDispute: (id, reason) => request("POST", `/claims/${id}/disputes`, { reason }),
    adjudicate: (id, reviewerNote) => request("POST", `/claims/${id}/adjudicate`, { reviewer_note: reviewerNote }),
    acceptPayment: (id) => request("POST", `/claims/${id}/accept`),
    getPayment: (id) => request("GET", `/claims/${id}/payment`),
};
```
